prompts.title: Draft Board Resolution for Bank Account Opening
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with 12 years of experience in corporate compliance under the Companies Act 2013. I need you to draft a comprehensive board resolution for opening a current account with HDFC Bank for a private limited company registered in Mumbai. Here is the context: ABC Technologies Private Limited (CIN: U72900MH2018PTC312456) wishes to open an operational current account at the HDFC Bank Fort Branch, Mumbai. The board consists of 4 directors, and the authorised signatories will be the Managing Director Mr. Rajesh Kumar and CFO Ms. Priya Sharma (jointly for transactions above INR 5 lakh, singly below that threshold). Please provide: (1) Full resolution text with standard opening clause referencing Section 179(3) of the Companies Act 2013, (2) Specific authorisation clauses for the bankers, (3) Signatory powers matrix with financial limits in INR, (4) Authority to execute bank mandate forms, KYC documents, and FATCA/CRS declarations, (5) Certified True Copy (CTC) format with attestation block. Format the output as a ready-to-print resolution on company letterhead, numbered clauses, with space for Chairperson signature. Consider constraints: must comply with MCA requirements, RBI KYC norms, and the company's Articles of Association. Include a note on filing requirements if applicable.

prompts.title: Annual Compliance Checklist for Private Limited Company
prompts.prompt: Act as a practising Company Secretary with 15 years of experience handling ROC compliances for SMEs in India. I need you to create a comprehensive annual compliance checklist for a private limited company with paid-up capital of INR 2 crore and turnover of INR 25 crore based in Bengaluru. Here is the context: The company's financial year ends 31 March 2026, and I need to plan all statutory compliances for FY 2025-26 covering the Companies Act 2013, Income Tax Act, GST, and labour laws. Please provide: (1) Month-wise compliance calendar from April 2025 to September 2026, (2) Each entry with form name (e.g., AOC-4, MGT-7, DIR-3 KYC, DPT-3, MSME-1), due date, applicable section, late fee/penalty, (3) Board meeting schedule as per Section 173 (minimum 4 meetings), (4) AGM timeline per Section 96, (5) Director disclosures (MBP-1, DIR-8), (6) Statutory registers to be maintained, (7) Event-based filings commonly triggered. Format the output as a structured table with columns: Month, Compliance Item, Form, Due Date, Section, Penalty. Consider constraints: MCA21 portal filing requirements, recent amendments to CFSS, and applicable exemptions for private companies.

prompts.title: Board Resolution for Borrowing Powers Under Section 180
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior Company Secretary with expertise in drafting corporate resolutions for listed and unlisted companies. I need you to draft a board resolution and corresponding special resolution for enhancing borrowing powers beyond paid-up capital and free reserves under Section 180(1)(c) of the Companies Act 2013. Here is the context: XYZ Infrastructure Limited (listed on BSE and NSE) has paid-up capital of INR 150 crore and free reserves of INR 280 crore. The board proposes to raise the borrowing limit to INR 1,500 crore to fund a highway project in Maharashtra. Please provide: (1) Board resolution recommending the enhancement to shareholders, (2) Draft special resolution for the EGM/AGM notice, (3) Explanatory statement under Section 102 covering project rationale, financial impact, and security creation, (4) Reference to existing borrowings and proposed lenders (SBI, HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank consortium), (5) SEBI LODR Regulation 30 disclosure requirements to stock exchanges, (6) Form MGT-14 filing note. Format the output as three separate documents: board resolution, explanatory statement, and SE intimation draft. Consider constraints: SEBI LODR 2015 disclosure timelines (within 24 hours of board approval), Companies Act Section 180, and listing agreement obligations.

prompts.title: Draft Resolution for Approval of Annual Accounts
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with 10 years of experience in board meeting administration. I need you to draft the board resolution for approval of audited financial statements, directors' report, and related documents for an Indian private limited company. Here is the context: Sunrise Retail Private Limited, Pune, has completed its statutory audit by M/s Sharma & Associates, Chartered Accountants, for FY 2024-25. Revenue is INR 85 crore, net profit INR 6.2 crore. The board meeting is scheduled for 28 May 2025. Please provide: (1) Resolution approving the standalone audited financial statements, (2) Approval of the Board's Report under Section 134, (3) Noting of statutory auditor's report and CARO 2020 observations, (4) Recommendation of dividend if any (INR 2 per share on 10 lakh equity shares), (5) Authorisation to sign the balance sheet under Section 134(1), (6) Authorisation for AGM notice issuance. Format the output as numbered agenda-wise resolutions with standard corporate language, including opening and closing clauses. Consider constraints: Section 134 signing requirements, Schedule III presentation, and AS/Ind AS applicability based on turnover thresholds.

prompts.title: Statutory Auditor Appointment Resolution and ADT-1
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary specialising in auditor appointment compliances under Chapter X of the Companies Act 2013. I need you to prepare the complete documentation package for appointing a new statutory auditor at the upcoming AGM. Here is the context: Metro Logistics Limited (unlisted public company), Chennai, is appointing M/s Krishnan & Co, Chartered Accountants (Firm Reg No. 001234S), as statutory auditor for a term of 5 years from FY 2025-26 to FY 2029-30, replacing the outgoing auditor whose tenure has ended. Please provide: (1) Board resolution recommending the appointment, (2) Written consent and eligibility certificate format under Section 139 and Section 141, (3) Draft ordinary resolution for the AGM notice with explanatory statement, (4) Form ADT-1 filing particulars and timeline (15 days), (5) Intimation letter to the new auditor, (6) Audit Committee recommendation note (Section 177). Format the output as a compliance pack with each item labelled and ready for execution. Consider constraints: auditor rotation requirements under Section 139(2), independence criteria under Section 141, and ICAI peer review certification.

prompts.title: CSR Committee Resolution and Policy Drafting
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with deep expertise in CSR compliance under Section 135 of the Companies Act 2013 and the Companies (CSR Policy) Rules 2014 as amended. I need you to draft board resolutions and a CSR policy framework for a qualifying company. Here is the context: GreenTech Industries Limited, Ahmedabad, has net profit of INR 52 crore (3-year average), triggering CSR applicability. Mandatory CSR spend: approximately INR 1.04 crore (2%). Focus areas proposed: education for underprivileged children, rural healthcare in Gujarat, and environmental sustainability. Please provide: (1) Board resolution constituting the CSR Committee with 3 directors including 1 independent director, (2) Terms of reference of the CSR Committee, (3) Board resolution approving the CSR Policy and Annual Action Plan, (4) CSR Policy document covering Schedule VII activities, implementation modes, monitoring, and impact assessment, (5) Resolution approving specific projects with budget allocation, (6) Unspent CSR amount treatment under Section 135(5) and 135(6). Format the output as a structured compliance document. Consider constraints: amended CSR rules, Form CSR-2 filing, and impact assessment threshold for INR 10 crore projects.

prompts.title: Investment Resolution Under Section 186
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with 14 years of experience in corporate compliance and inter-corporate investments. I need you to draft a board resolution for an investment in equity shares of a subsidiary company, ensuring Section 186 compliance. Here is the context: Apex Holdings Limited proposes to invest INR 75 crore in acquiring 60% equity stake of Apex Fintech Private Limited (new subsidiary). Apex Holdings has paid-up capital of INR 200 crore, free reserves of INR 450 crore, and existing investments of INR 180 crore. Please provide: (1) Calculation of Section 186 limits (60% of paid-up + free reserves OR 100% of free reserves + securities premium), (2) Board resolution with unanimous consent requirement, (3) Audit Committee approval note, (4) Special resolution draft if limits exceeded, (5) Register of investments entry format (Form MBP-2), (6) MGT-14 filing requirement, (7) Valuation report reference as per Rule 11UA/Section 247. Format the output as a compliance memo with the resolution appended. Consider constraints: Section 186 ceilings, Rule 11 of Companies (Meetings of Board) Rules 2014, and applicable FEMA provisions if foreign involvement.

prompts.title: Inter-Corporate Loan Resolution and Documentation
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior Company Secretary specialising in inter-corporate transactions and Section 185/186 compliance. I need you to prepare complete documentation for an inter-corporate loan between group companies. Here is the context: Parent Co (Bhagat Industries Limited) proposes to grant an unsecured loan of INR 40 crore to its wholly-owned subsidiary (Bhagat Exports Private Limited) at 9% p.a. interest for working capital, repayable in 3 years. Please provide: (1) Board resolution of the lending company under Section 186, (2) Board resolution of the borrowing company accepting the loan, (3) Loan agreement key clauses (rate, tenure, security, events of default), (4) Section 185 analysis and applicability check, (5) Interest rate benchmarking against prevailing yield (G-sec plus premium), (6) Disclosure in Board's Report under Section 134(3)(g), (7) Register of loans format (MBP-2). Format the output as a transaction pack with each document clearly labelled. Consider constraints: Section 185 exemption for wholly-owned subsidiaries, arm's length pricing under transfer pricing regulations, and MCA notification for loan rates not below G-sec yield.

prompts.title: SEBI LODR Regulation 30 Event Disclosure Drafting
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary and Compliance Officer of a listed company with 12 years of experience in SEBI LODR compliance. I need you to draft Regulation 30 event-based disclosures to the stock exchanges. Here is the context: Luminous Motors Limited (listed on BSE: 532XXX and NSE: LUMMOT) has today approved: (1) acquisition of 100% stake in a Pune-based EV battery startup for INR 320 crore, (2) resignation of the CFO Mr. Ashok Mehta effective 30 June 2025, (3) appointment of new CFO Ms. Neelam Gupta, and (4) incorporation of a new wholly-owned subsidiary in Germany. Please provide: (1) Four separate disclosure letters addressed to BSE and NSE, (2) Compliance with Part A of Schedule III materiality criteria, (3) Timeline check (30 minutes for board decisions, 24 hours for other events), (4) Specific disclosures required for CFO change (Annexure on brief profile, reasons, relationships), (5) Reference to company's materiality policy, (6) Format per SEBI circular dated July 2023 and subsequent amendments. Format the output as ready-to-upload intimation letters on company letterhead. Consider constraints: SEBI LODR 2015 Regulation 30, Schedule III Part A, and recent SEBI circulars on materiality thresholds.

prompts.title: AGM Notice Drafting for Private Company
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary experienced in drafting AGM notices compliant with the Companies Act 2013 and Secretarial Standard SS-2. I need you to draft the AGM notice for a private limited company with 8 items of business. Here is the context: Vikram Enterprises Private Limited, Hyderabad, is holding its 12th AGM on Friday, 26 September 2025, at 11:00 AM at the registered office. Ordinary business: adoption of accounts, dividend declaration (INR 3 per share), reappointment of Ms. Kavitha Rao as retiring director. Special business: reappointment of auditor for 5 years, ratification of related party transactions with associate company, adoption of new Articles, approval of ESOP 2025 scheme, increase in managerial remuneration. Please provide: (1) Complete AGM notice with convening paragraph, agenda items, routes of participation, (2) Explanatory statement under Section 102 for each special business item, (3) Proxy form (MGT-11), (4) Attendance slip, (5) Route map to venue, (6) Notes on e-voting if applicable, (7) Book closure dates. Format the output as a ready-to-print notice package following SS-2. Consider constraints: 21 clear days notice period, Section 101/102/105 requirements, and SS-2 compliance.

prompts.title: MGT-7 Annual Return Preparation Guide
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with 15 years of experience in MCA21 filings and annual return preparation. I need you to create a detailed guide and checklist for preparing Form MGT-7 for a private limited company. Here is the context: Nexus Software Solutions Private Limited, Noida, with paid-up capital INR 5 crore, 45 shareholders, 5 directors, and 2 subsidiaries, needs to file MGT-7 for FY 2024-25 by 28 November 2025 (60 days from AGM held on 29 September 2025). Please provide: (1) Section-wise data requirements for MGT-7 (registered office, principal activities, particulars of holding/subsidiary/associate companies, share capital, debentures, turnover, members, directors, KMPs, meetings, remuneration, penalties, compliance certificate), (2) Supporting documents checklist, (3) Common errors to avoid (e.g., SRN mismatches, signatory DIN issues), (4) Pre-scrutiny checks on MCA21 portal, (5) Certification by practising CS if turnover exceeds INR 50 crore or paid-up exceeds INR 10 crore (Form MGT-8), (6) Late filing penalty calculation (INR 100 per day). Format the output as a step-by-step checklist with timelines. Consider constraints: Rule 11 of Companies (Management and Administration) Rules 2014, and digital signature requirements.

prompts.title: AOC-4 XBRL Filing Preparation Checklist
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with expertise in XBRL filings and Ind AS reporting on MCA21. I need you to create a comprehensive checklist for AOC-4 XBRL filing. Here is the context: Orbit Pharma Limited, Mumbai, is an unlisted public company with turnover INR 650 crore and borrowings INR 200 crore, mandatorily falling under Ind AS and XBRL filing requirement. Financial statements for FY 2024-25 are ready; AGM held on 28 September 2025; AOC-4 XBRL due by 27 October 2025. Please provide: (1) Applicability check for XBRL filing (Ind AS companies, listed entities, companies meeting threshold criteria), (2) Taxonomy and instance document preparation steps, (3) Attachments checklist (financials, auditor's report, board report, CSR report, secretarial audit, Form ADT-1), (4) Pre-validation on MCA portal, (5) Common XBRL errors and fixes, (6) Certification requirements (CA/CS/CMA), (7) Penalty for delayed filing (INR 100 per day), (8) Linkage with AOC-4 CFS for holding companies. Format the output as a sequential action plan with responsible parties and deadlines. Consider constraints: MCA XBRL filing manual, Ind AS taxonomy, and recent validation updates.

prompts.title: Related Party Transaction Audit Committee Memo
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary and Compliance Officer experienced in related party transactions under Section 188 of the Companies Act 2013 and SEBI LODR Regulation 23. I need you to draft a complete memo for Audit Committee approval of RPTs. Here is the context: Sunflower FMCG Limited (listed) proposes to enter into: (1) sale of products worth INR 85 crore to its subsidiary Sunflower Foods Private Limited, (2) availing management services of INR 12 crore per annum from promoter-owned entity Sunflower Holdings LLP, (3) rental of office premises at Andheri for INR 2.5 crore per annum from director's HUF. Please provide: (1) Nature of each transaction with parties, amounts, and durations, (2) Arm's length justification with benchmarking, (3) Materiality analysis under LODR Reg 23 (10% of turnover threshold), (4) Audit Committee approval resolution, (5) Board recommendation, (6) Shareholder approval requirement if material, (7) Disclosure in Board's Report (AOC-2) and quarterly RPT disclosure to exchanges. Format the output as an Audit Committee agenda memo. Consider constraints: Section 188, LODR Reg 23 as amended, Ind AS 24, and SEBI disclosure format for H1/H2.

prompts.title: Postal Ballot Notice and Process
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary of a listed company with extensive experience in conducting postal ballots via electronic voting. I need you to draft a postal ballot notice and process document. Here is the context: Horizon Cements Limited (NSE listed) proposes three items requiring shareholder approval through postal ballot: (1) alteration of object clause of Memorandum of Association to add renewable energy business, (2) increase in borrowing limit under Section 180(1)(c) from INR 800 crore to INR 1,500 crore, (3) appointment of Mr. Arjun Kapoor as independent director. Please provide: (1) Postal ballot notice as per Section 110 and Rule 22 of Companies (Management and Administration) Rules, (2) Three special resolutions with explanatory statements, (3) Cut-off date determination, (4) e-voting timelines (minimum 30 days from despatch), (5) Scrutinizer appointment and format of report, (6) Results announcement to exchanges within 2 working days, (7) MGT-14 filing post approval. Format the output as a postal ballot dispatch pack. Consider constraints: Section 110, SS-2, MCA circulars on e-voting, SEBI LODR Regulation 44, and NSDL/CDSL e-voting framework.

prompts.title: Dividend Declaration and Payment Compliance
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with 10 years of experience managing dividend compliances for Indian companies. I need you to prepare a complete dividend declaration and payment roadmap. Here is the context: Pinnacle Textiles Limited (listed on BSE) proposes final dividend of INR 4 per share on 5 crore equity shares of INR 10 each (total outflow INR 20 crore) for FY 2024-25. AGM scheduled 20 September 2025. Please provide: (1) Board resolution recommending dividend, (2) Transfer to separate dividend bank account within 5 days of AGM declaration (Section 123(4)), (3) Payment to shareholders within 30 days, (4) TDS compliance under Section 194 (10% for resident individuals above INR 5000), (5) Unpaid Dividend Account opening after 30 days, (6) Transfer to IEPF after 7 years for both amounts and underlying shares (IEPF-5, IEPF-4), (7) Disclosure to stock exchanges, record date and book closure intimation, (8) Form IEPF-2 annual filing. Format the output as a day-wise action plan with responsibilities. Consider constraints: Companies (Declaration and Payment of Dividend) Rules, IEPF Rules 2016, and SEBI LODR Regulation 42.

prompts.title: Event-Based ROC Filings Mapping Tool
prompts.prompt: Act as a practising Company Secretary with deep knowledge of MCA e-forms and event-based compliance. I need you to create a comprehensive mapping of events to corresponding ROC filings for a private limited company. Here is the context: Alpine Foods Private Limited, Dehradun, anticipates multiple corporate actions in FY 2025-26 including appointment of new director, change of registered office within same state, increase in authorised capital from INR 1 crore to INR 5 crore, allotment of shares on preferential basis, creation of charge on new term loan of INR 15 crore from Axis Bank, appointment of auditor to fill casual vacancy, and conversion to public limited company. Please provide: (1) Event-to-form mapping table (Event | Form | Section/Rule | Time limit | Fees | Attachments), covering DIR-12, INC-22, SH-7, PAS-3, CHG-1, ADT-1, INC-27, (2) Pre-filing approvals required (board/shareholder), (3) Post-filing compliances, (4) Common rejection reasons on MCA21, (5) Late fee structure under Section 403. Format the output as a reference matrix table. Consider constraints: current MCA fee rules, V3 portal requirements, and updated DSC protocols.

prompts.title: SEBI LODR Quarterly Compliance Report Drafting
prompts.prompt: Act as a Compliance Officer of a listed company with 12 years of experience in SEBI LODR quarterly filings. I need you to prepare the quarterly compliance report under Regulation 27(2) of SEBI LODR 2015 for the quarter ended 30 June 2025. Here is the context: Crystal Glass Limited (BSE listed, market cap INR 1,200 crore) has a 7-member board with 4 independent directors including one woman independent director, Audit Committee, NRC, Stakeholder Relationship Committee, and Risk Management Committee. Q1 FY26 results declared on 12 August 2025. Please provide: (1) Corporate Governance report in SEBI prescribed format, (2) Details of board and committee meetings held during the quarter, (3) Composition compliance check, (4) Shareholder grievance status, (5) Affirmations on related party transactions, insider trading code compliance, and secretarial compliance, (6) Signing by Compliance Officer and submission timeline (21 days from quarter end), (7) Compliance certificate by PCS under Reg 7(3) for share transfer agent, (8) Format for BSE/NSE upload. Format the output as a filing-ready report. Consider constraints: SEBI LODR 2015, amendments via 2021/2023 circulars, and exchange-specific filing portals.

prompts.title: Board Meeting Minutes Drafting Template
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with 15 years of experience in drafting board meeting minutes compliant with Secretarial Standard SS-1. I need you to draft comprehensive minutes for a quarterly board meeting. Here is the context: Elite Engineering Limited held its Q1 FY26 board meeting on 12 August 2025 at 11:30 AM at the registered office in Gurugram. Agenda covered: Q1 unaudited financial results approval, limited review report noting, appointment of internal auditor, investment proposal of INR 60 crore in new plant in Bhiwadi, CSR project approval, noting of statutory dues, SEBI LODR compliance report. 6 of 7 directors attended (1 joined via video conferencing). Please provide: (1) Header with meeting number, date, time, venue, mode, (2) Attendance table with names and designations, (3) Confirmation of quorum under Section 174, (4) Chairperson and invitee details, (5) Leave of absence noting, (6) Agenda-wise minutes with background, discussion, and resolution, (7) Any other business, (8) Vote of thanks and closure, (9) Signature block. Format the output as ready-to-circulate draft minutes per SS-1. Consider constraints: SS-1, Section 118, 30-day circulation requirement, and video conferencing rules.

prompts.title: Independent Director Appointment Compliance Pack
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary specialising in board composition and independent director compliances. I need you to prepare the complete compliance pack for appointing an independent director on a listed company board. Here is the context: Radiant Chemicals Limited (NSE listed) proposes to appoint Dr. Meena Iyer, former IIT Madras professor and current board member of two other listed companies, as an Independent Director for a first term of 5 years effective 1 October 2025. Please provide: (1) NRC recommendation note and resolution, (2) Declaration of independence under Section 149(6), (3) DIR-2 consent and DIR-8 disclosure, (4) Verification of IICA registration and ID proficiency test status, (5) Board resolution recommending appointment, (6) Special resolution for AGM/postal ballot, (7) Letter of appointment with terms and conditions as per Schedule IV, (8) Familiarisation programme outline, (9) Stock exchange intimation under LODR Reg 30, (10) Form DIR-12 filing. Format the output as a step-by-step compliance package. Consider constraints: Section 149, Schedule IV, SEBI LODR Reg 17 and 25, IICA databank requirements, and recent tenure restrictions.

prompts.title: DIR-3 KYC Compliance Tracker
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary handling DIN-related compliances for a group of companies. I need you to build a DIR-3 KYC compliance tracker and communication template. Here is the context: Tata-style conglomerate Zenith Group has 18 operating companies with 46 directors holding DINs. DIR-3 KYC is due annually by 30 September, with penalty of INR 5,000 per defaulting DIN and DIN deactivation. Some directors are NRIs with foreign addresses. Please provide: (1) Master tracker template (Director name, DIN, DOB, mobile, email, address, citizenship, DSC validity, last filed date, current year status), (2) Communication letter to directors requesting KYC data and OTP-based verification, (3) Distinction between DIR-3 KYC (web) and DIR-3 KYC eForm (for changes), (4) NRI-specific document checklist (apostilled documents, overseas address proof), (5) Deactivation restoration process if missed, (6) Aadhaar OTP authentication workflow, (7) Bulk filing strategy for group companies. Format the output as a tracker template plus operational SOP. Consider constraints: Rule 12A of Companies (Appointment and Qualification of Directors) Rules, MCA V3 portal, and DPDP Act 2023 data handling.

prompts.title: Preferential Allotment Resolution and PAS-3
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with expertise in equity fundraising for unlisted companies under Section 62(1)(c) of the Companies Act 2013. I need you to draft the complete documentation for a preferential allotment of equity shares. Here is the context: Fintonic Private Limited, Bengaluru, proposes to issue 5,00,000 equity shares of face value INR 10 at a premium of INR 290 (total INR 15 crore) to Blue Ocean Ventures (Singapore-based fund) as Series B funding. Please provide: (1) Board resolution approving the offer, (2) Valuation report reference under Rule 13 of Companies (Share Capital and Debentures) Rules by Registered Valuer, (3) Explanatory statement under Section 102 covering price justification, utilisation plan, and dilution, (4) Special resolution for EGM, (5) PAS-4 offer letter, (6) Board resolution for allotment post subscription receipt, (7) PAS-3 return of allotment within 15 days, (8) Share certificate issuance and stamp duty (Indian Stamp Act), (9) FC-GPR filing under FEMA for non-resident investor. Format the output as a sequenced compliance document pack. Consider constraints: Rule 13, Section 42 vs Section 62 distinction, valuation under Rule 11UA, and FEMA NDI Rules 2019.

prompts.title: Secretarial Audit Report MR-3 Draft
prompts.prompt: Act as a Practising Company Secretary with COP empanelled for secretarial audits under Section 204 of the Companies Act 2013. I need you to draft a secretarial audit report in Form MR-3 for a listed company. Here is the context: Everest Infra Limited (BSE listed), Mumbai, with paid-up capital INR 300 crore, requires secretarial audit for FY 2024-25. Entity is engaged in road construction across Maharashtra and Gujarat. Please provide: (1) Opening paragraph with scope and period, (2) List of laws examined (Companies Act 2013, SCRA, Depositories Act, FEMA, SEBI Regulations including LODR, PIT, SAST, ICDR, Buyback, DP, labour laws, environmental laws, sector-specific laws), (3) Compliance certification on each, (4) Board composition observations, (5) Adequate systems and processes comment, (6) Specific observations/qualifications with impact, (7) Annexure A management responsibility, (8) PCS name, ACS/FCS number, CP number, UDIN, peer review. Format the output as a signing-ready MR-3 report. Consider constraints: Section 204, Rule 9 of Companies (Appointment and Remuneration) Rules 2014, ICSI Auditing Standards (CSAS), and UDIN requirements.

prompts.title: Charge Creation and Satisfaction Filing Guide
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary handling charge filings and security interest registrations for a corporate group. I need you to create a complete guide for charge creation (CHG-1) and satisfaction (CHG-4) filings. Here is the context: Olive Ventures Limited has availed a term loan of INR 80 crore from State Bank of India secured by hypothecation of plant and machinery at its Chennai facility and mortgage over Bengaluru office property. Separately, an earlier loan of INR 25 crore from ICICI Bank has been fully repaid and requires charge satisfaction. Please provide: (1) CHG-1 filing checklist with attachments (instrument of charge, board resolution, valuation, NOC), 30-day filing timeline with extended 300-day option on payment of additional fees, (2) Registration of charge in Register of Charges (CHG-7), (3) CHG-9 for debentures, (4) CHG-4 satisfaction filing within 30 days of repayment with NOC from lender, (5) Memorandum of satisfaction (CHG-8 for condonation if delayed), (6) Impact on CERSAI and SARFAESI registration, (7) Disclosure in Board's Report. Format the output as a procedural SOP with form flow diagram. Consider constraints: Sections 77-87 of Companies Act 2013, Rule 4 of Companies (Registration of Charges) Rules, and CERSAI cross-filing.

prompts.title: Nomination and Remuneration Committee Policy
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with experience advising NRCs of listed companies under Section 178 and SEBI LODR Regulation 19. I need you to draft a comprehensive NRC policy. Here is the context: Skyline Steel Limited (NSE listed) is formalising its NRC policy covering selection criteria, remuneration of directors and senior management, board diversity, and succession planning. Please provide: (1) Constitution and composition of NRC (minimum 3 non-executive directors, 2/3rd independent), (2) Role and terms of reference, (3) Criteria for identification of directors (qualifications, positive attributes, independence), (4) Remuneration policy covering executive directors, non-executive directors (sitting fees up to INR 1 lakh per meeting), independent directors (commission up to 1% of profits), KMPs and senior management, (5) Board diversity policy including gender diversity, (6) Performance evaluation framework under Reg 17(10), (7) Succession planning for board and senior management, (8) Review and disclosure in Board's Report. Format the output as a board-approved policy document. Consider constraints: Section 178, Schedule IV, SEBI LODR Reg 17, 19, and 36, and MCA rules on sitting fees.

prompts.title: DPT-3 Return of Deposits Preparation
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary familiar with deposit-related filings under Chapter V of the Companies Act 2013. I need you to prepare a DPT-3 filing guide for a private limited company. Here is the context: Lotus Manufacturing Private Limited, Kolkata, has outstanding borrowings as on 31 March 2025 comprising: unsecured loans from directors INR 3 crore, loans from holding company INR 8 crore, term loan from Yes Bank INR 20 crore, and commercial papers INR 5 crore. DPT-3 is due by 30 June 2025. Please provide: (1) Classification of each borrowing as deposit or exempted deposit under Rule 2(1)(c) of Companies (Acceptance of Deposits) Rules 2014, (2) DPT-3 applicability (one-time return vs annual return), (3) Data points required (outstanding amount, purpose, source, tenor, rate), (4) Auditor's certificate requirement, (5) Director's declaration on non-deposit nature, (6) Attachments checklist, (7) Filing fees, (8) Penalty for non-filing, (9) Linkage with MSME-1 filing. Format the output as a preparation worksheet with classification matrix. Consider constraints: Rule 16A of Deposit Rules, MCA circulars on exempted deposits, and recent amendments.

prompts.title: Shareholder Communication for Buyback Offer
prompts.prompt: Act as a Compliance Officer of a listed company with experience in share buyback transactions under the Companies Act 2013 and SEBI Buyback Regulations 2018. I need you to draft the shareholder communication pack for a buyback offer through tender route. Here is the context: Monarch Technologies Limited (NSE: MONTECH) proposes buyback of up to 50 lakh equity shares at INR 850 per share (aggregate INR 425 crore) through tender offer route from all existing shareholders, including reserved portion for small shareholders. Please provide: (1) Board resolution and public announcement under Reg 7, (2) Letter of offer key contents, (3) Draft public advertisement in newspapers, (4) Tender form for shareholders, (5) Email/SMS communication to retail shareholders, (6) Promoter undertaking, (7) Acceptance and payment timeline, (8) Post-buyback corporate actions (extinguishment of shares, SH-11), (9) Escrow account details and deposit, (10) SEBI filings and fees. Format the output as a communication pack with templates. Consider constraints: SEBI Buyback Regulations 2018, Section 68 of Companies Act, Reg 10 of Buyback Regs (debt-equity ratio 2:1), and stock exchange notification formats.

prompts.title: Annual Performance Evaluation Framework
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with experience in board governance and performance evaluation for listed companies. I need you to design an annual board performance evaluation framework. Here is the context: Peacock Paints Limited (BSE listed), Ahmedabad, needs to conduct performance evaluation of the board, committees, individual directors including independent directors, and the Chairperson for FY 2024-25 under Section 178(2) and SEBI LODR Reg 17(10). Please provide: (1) Evaluation methodology (self-evaluation + peer evaluation + external facilitator option), (2) Questionnaires for board, audit committee, NRC, SRC, individual directors, Chairperson, and independent directors (separate meeting under Schedule IV), (3) Rating scale (1-5) with parameters covering strategy, risk oversight, composition, functioning, information flow, governance, (4) NRC review and recommendations process, (5) Outcome disclosure in Board's Report (specific format prescribed), (6) Action plan for identified gaps, (7) Confidentiality protocols. Format the output as an evaluation toolkit with questionnaires. Consider constraints: Section 178, Schedule IV, SEBI LODR Reg 17(10) and 25(4), and SEBI Guidance Note on Board Evaluation 2017.

prompts.title: Resolution for Related Party Sale of Assets
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with expertise in Section 180 and Section 188 intersection for asset sales to related parties. I need you to draft the compliance documents. Here is the context: Galaxy Holdings Limited (unlisted public company) proposes to sell its non-core manufacturing undertaking at Vapi (gross block INR 180 crore, book value INR 110 crore) to its wholly-owned subsidiary Galaxy Manufacturing Limited for INR 145 crore based on valuation report. This constitutes sale of substantial undertaking triggering Section 180(1)(a). Please provide: (1) Audit Committee approval resolution under Section 177 and 188, (2) Board resolution under Section 180(1)(a) and Section 188, (3) Explanatory statement with valuation, financial impact, rationale, (4) Special resolution for shareholders, (5) Slump sale agreement key terms (Section 2(42C) Income Tax), (6) Section 188 exemption check for WOS transactions on arm's length basis, (7) Stamp duty implications state-wise, (8) MGT-14 filing, (9) Disclosure in Board's Report (AOC-2). Format the output as a transaction compliance memo. Consider constraints: 'substantial undertaking' 20% threshold, arm's length requirement, and Section 2(42C) slump sale definition.

prompts.title: Insider Trading Code Compliance Framework
prompts.prompt: Act as a Compliance Officer of a listed company designated under SEBI PIT Regulations 2015. I need you to develop the complete insider trading compliance framework. Here is the context: Stellar Biotech Limited (NSE listed) has 45 designated persons including directors, KMPs, senior management, and connected persons. Company needs to implement Code of Conduct, structured digital database, trading window mechanism, and pre-clearance process. Please provide: (1) Code of Conduct under Schedule B of PIT Regulations, (2) Trading window closure calendar linked to financial results and UPSI events, (3) Pre-clearance application format and approval matrix (transactions above INR 10 lakh require approval), (4) Disclosures under Reg 7 (initial, continual, annual) with Form A/B/C/D formats, (5) Structured digital database maintenance (UPSI sharing log with PAN and purpose), (6) Contra-trade restriction (6 months), (7) Code of Fair Disclosure under Schedule A, (8) Reporting violations to SEBI and Audit Committee, (9) Annual certification by CO. Format the output as a PIT compliance manual. Consider constraints: SEBI (PIT) Regulations 2015 as amended, 2022 database amendment, and Reg 9A internal controls.

prompts.title: Notice of EGM Via Video Conferencing
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary experienced in conducting general meetings via VC/OAVM. I need you to draft a complete EGM notice for a listed company held via video conferencing. Here is the context: Coral Pharma Limited needs to convene an EGM on 15 November 2025 via VC/OAVM to seek shareholder approval for: (1) raising funds up to INR 500 crore through QIP, (2) increase in foreign investment limit from 49% to 74%, (3) amendment to Articles of Association. Please provide: (1) EGM notice with convening paragraph mentioning VC/OAVM mode and legal basis (MCA general circulars + SEBI circulars), (2) Three special resolutions with explanatory statements, (3) Instructions for participation via e-voting platform (NSDL/CDSL), (4) Procedure for speaker registration, (5) Remote e-voting timelines, (6) Deemed venue clarification, (7) Scrutinizer appointment, (8) Stock exchange intimation, (9) Post-meeting filings (MGT-14, results to exchanges within 2 working days). Format the output as a VC-enabled EGM notice pack. Consider constraints: latest MCA general circulars on VC meetings, SEBI LODR Reg 44, and SS-2 applicability with VC carve-outs.

prompts.title: Compliance Certificate by PCS for Annual Return
prompts.prompt: Act as a Practising Company Secretary authorised to certify annual returns under Section 92(2). I need you to draft the MGT-8 compliance certificate. Here is the context: Trident Engineering Limited (unlisted public company), Pune, with paid-up capital INR 18 crore and turnover INR 280 crore, requires MGT-8 certification from PCS for FY 2024-25. Please provide: (1) Applicability trigger (listed company OR paid-up capital INR 10 crore+ OR turnover INR 50 crore+), (2) Full MGT-8 format with opening paragraph, (3) Verification points covering maintenance of statutory registers, filing of forms and returns with ROC, status of board and general meetings, closure of registers of members, forms filed with other regulators (SEBI, RBI), compliance with specific provisions (Sections 42, 62, 73, 77, 178, 188, 197), penalties if any, (4) Qualifications/observations format, (5) PCS details (name, membership number FCS/ACS, CP number, UDIN, peer review number), (6) Issuance date and place. Format the output as a signing-ready MGT-8 certificate. Consider constraints: Rule 11 of Companies (Management and Administration) Rules, ICSI guidelines, and UDIN mandate.

prompts.title: Directors Report Drafting with Annexures
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with 12 years of experience drafting Directors' Reports for Indian companies. I need you to prepare a comprehensive Directors' Report for a mid-sized listed company. Here is the context: Valor Automobiles Limited (BSE listed) has turnover INR 1,850 crore and net profit INR 142 crore for FY 2024-25. Company has 2 subsidiaries, 1 associate, operations across 4 states, CSR applicability, ESOP scheme, independent directors, and material related party transactions. Please provide: (1) Full Directors' Report structure under Section 134(3), (2) Financial highlights and state of company affairs, (3) Dividend recommendation and reserves, (4) Changes in share capital, (5) Subsidiaries/associates (AOC-1 reference), (6) Directors and KMP changes, (7) Number of board meetings, (8) Board evaluation, (9) Declaration by independent directors, (10) Remuneration details (Rule 5 disclosure), (11) CSR Report annexure (amended rules), (12) Secretarial audit annexure (MR-3), (13) Conservation of energy and tech absorption (Rule 8(3)), (14) Risk management, (15) Related party transactions (AOC-2), (16) Responsibility statement under Section 134(5). Format the output as a draft report with labelled sections. Consider constraints: Section 134, Rule 8 of Companies (Accounts) Rules, and SEBI LODR Schedule V.

prompts.title: Corporate Governance Report for Annual Report
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary and Compliance Officer preparing the Corporate Governance Report under Schedule V Part C of SEBI LODR. I need you to draft the full CG report for inclusion in the annual report. Here is the context: Oasis Hotels Limited (NSE listed) needs to publish its CG report for FY 2024-25 covering board composition, committees, general meetings, disclosures, and compliance affirmations. Board has 9 directors (4 independent, 1 woman ID). Please provide: (1) Company's philosophy on corporate governance, (2) Board of Directors table with category, DIN, attendance, other directorships, committee memberships, (3) Audit Committee composition, terms of reference, meetings, attendance, (4) NRC details, (5) Stakeholders Relationship Committee, (6) Risk Management Committee, (7) Remuneration of directors with disclosure table, (8) General body meetings (last 3 years) and special resolutions, (9) Means of communication, (10) General shareholder information (AGM venue, financial calendar, listing, stock data, distribution of shareholding, dematerialisation, plant locations, registrar), (11) Disclosures (RPT, penalties, whistle-blower, commodity risk, material subsidiaries), (12) PCS certificate on CG compliance, (13) CEO/CFO certification. Format the output as a ready-for-annual-report document. Consider constraints: SEBI LODR Schedule V and Reg 34.

prompts.title: Resolution Plan for Defaulting ROC Filings
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with experience in regularising defaulting companies through CFSS-like schemes and Section 460 condonation. I need you to prepare a remediation plan for a company with multiple ROC defaults. Here is the context: Prime Traders Private Limited, Jaipur, has not filed AOC-4 for FY 2022-23, MGT-7 for FY 2022-23 and FY 2023-24, DIR-3 KYC for 2 directors, and DPT-3 for 2 years. Company wants to regularise all defaults and resume active status. Please provide: (1) Default inventory with form name, due date, delay period, (2) Late fee calculation (INR 100 per day) for each form, (3) Filing sequence and dependency map, (4) Director DIN reactivation via DIR-3 KYC and penalty payment INR 5,000 per DIN, (5) Condonation of delay application under Section 460 if required, (6) Strike-off risk assessment under Section 248 and restoration via NCLT if applicable (Section 252), (7) Compounding applications under Section 441 for any substantive non-compliances, (8) Post-regularisation preventive compliance calendar. Format the output as a remediation roadmap with budget and timeline. Consider constraints: MCA V3 portal, Section 403 additional fees, and recent MCA adjudication orders.

prompts.title: Quarterly Shareholding Pattern Filing
prompts.prompt: Act as a Compliance Officer of a listed company preparing the quarterly shareholding pattern under SEBI LODR Regulation 31. I need you to prepare the shareholding pattern filing for the quarter ended 30 September 2025. Here is the context: Riverstone Industries Limited (BSE and NSE listed) has total paid-up capital of 12 crore equity shares. Promoter holding 48%, FII/FPI 15%, MFs 8%, insurance companies 4%, retail 20%, HUF 2%, NRIs 3%. One promoter has pledged 20% of his holding. Please provide: (1) Format I (Summary), Format II (Promoter/promoter group), Format III (Public shareholding), Format IV (Non-promoter non-public) as per SEBI prescribed format, (2) Disclosure of encumbered shares under Reg 31(1), (3) Classification of promoter vs public shareholders, (4) Reconciliation with depositories and RTA, (5) Changes from previous quarter, (6) Timeline (21 days from quarter end), (7) Filing mode on BSE Listing Centre and NSE NEAPS portals, (8) Display on company website under Reg 46. Format the output as a filing-ready pattern with tables. Consider constraints: SEBI LODR Reg 31 and 31A, SEBI SAST Regulations for threshold triggers, and RTA reconciliation protocol.

prompts.title: Reconciliation of Share Capital Audit Report
prompts.prompt: Act as a Practising Company Secretary empanelled for Reconciliation of Share Capital Audit under SEBI circulars. I need you to draft the quarterly RSCA report for a listed company. Here is the context: Diamond Finance Limited (NSE listed) has issued capital of 8 crore equity shares of INR 10 each. For the quarter ended 30 June 2025, the PCS needs to certify reconciliation between issued capital, listed capital, and capital held in dematerialised and physical forms. Please provide: (1) Scope and purpose of RSCA under SEBI (D&P) Regulations 1996 and SEBI LODR, (2) Reconciliation table (Issued capital, listed capital, NSDL holding, CDSL holding, physical holding, total demat + physical), (3) Identification of mismatch and corrective actions, (4) Verification of in-transit shares, (5) Demat requests pending beyond 21 days, (6) Changes in capital during the quarter, (7) Compliance with Reg 76 of D&P, (8) PCS observations and qualifications, (9) Submission to BSE/NSE within 30 days of quarter end, (10) Signing block with UDIN. Format the output as a signing-ready RSCA report. Consider constraints: SEBI (D&P) Regulations, SEBI LODR Reg 40 and 55A, and depository bye-laws.

prompts.title: Resolution for Managerial Remuneration Approval
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary with expertise in managerial remuneration compliances under Section 197 and Schedule V of the Companies Act 2013. I need you to draft resolutions and calculations for managerial remuneration approval. Here is the context: Jasmine Foods Limited (listed, profits INR 45 crore FY25) proposes to pay Mr. Vikram Shah as Managing Director INR 2.8 crore (salary + perquisites + commission) and Ms. Anita Desai as Whole-Time Director INR 1.9 crore for FY 2025-26. Total managerial remuneration is 10.4% of net profits calculated under Section 198. Please provide: (1) Section 198 net profit computation with additions and deductions, (2) Overall 11% ceiling and individual 5% ceiling check, (3) NRC recommendation note, (4) Board resolution approving the remuneration, (5) Special resolution if exceeding Schedule V Part II limits, (6) Explanatory statement with prescribed particulars (Item III of Part II), (7) Central Government approval requirement test (no longer required post 2018 amendment except in inadequate profit cases beyond Schedule V), (8) Disclosure in Board's Report under Rule 5, (9) MR-1 filing within 60 days. Format the output as a compliance pack. Consider constraints: Section 197, 198, Schedule V Part II, and 2020 amendments on NED remuneration in inadequate profits.

prompts.title: DPDP Act Compliance Framework for Company Secretary
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary and Data Protection Officer implementing the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 for a corporate entity. I need you to create a DPDP compliance framework focused on secretarial and shareholder data. Here is the context: Canopy Retail Limited (listed), Delhi, processes personal data of 75,000 shareholders, 1,200 employees, 350 directors/KMPs across group, and vendors. As a Data Fiduciary, the company must comply with DPDP Act 2023 consent, notice, and security obligations. Please provide: (1) Data inventory mapping for CS function (shareholder registers, director KYC, board papers, meeting minutes, email disclosures), (2) Privacy notice for shareholders and directors, (3) Consent management process under Section 6, (4) Data retention policy aligned with Companies Act requirements (8 years for registers), (5) Security safeguards under Section 8, (6) Data breach notification process to DPB, (7) Grievance redressal mechanism, (8) Rights of Data Principals (access, correction, erasure, nomination), (9) DPO appointment if SDF, (10) Board-level governance and reporting. Format the output as a compliance framework document. Consider constraints: DPDP Act 2023, draft DPDP Rules, Companies Act data retention requirements, and SEBI PIT database.

prompts.title: Post-AGM Filing Checklist for Listed Company
prompts.prompt: Act as a Compliance Officer of a listed company with 10 years of experience in post-AGM filings. I need you to create a comprehensive post-AGM filing checklist. Here is the context: Meridian Capital Limited (NSE listed) held its 28th AGM on 25 September 2025 where shareholders approved adoption of accounts, dividend of INR 5 per share, reappointment of 2 retiring directors, appointment of statutory auditor, and three special resolutions (ESOP scheme, RPT ratification, borrowing limit increase). Please provide: (1) Voting results intimation to stock exchanges within 2 working days (Reg 44), (2) Scrutinizer's report upload, (3) MGT-14 filings for special resolutions within 30 days, (4) MGT-15 report on AGM within 30 days (for listed companies), (5) AOC-4 and AOC-4 CFS within 30 days, (6) MGT-7 within 60 days, (7) ADT-1 for auditor appointment within 15 days, (8) Dividend payment within 30 days and unpaid dividend transfer, (9) Secretarial compliance report under Reg 24A within 60 days, (10) Updating of website under Reg 46 with AGM recording. Format the output as a 60-day action timeline with forms, sections, and owners. Consider constraints: SEBI LODR 2015, Companies Act 2013, and MCA timelines.

prompts.title: ICDR Fundraise Compliance Memo for QIP
prompts.prompt: Act as a Company Secretary and Compliance Officer with experience in equity fundraising under SEBI ICDR Regulations 2018. I need you to draft a comprehensive compliance memo for a Qualified Institutions Placement. Here is the context: Falcon Infrastructure Limited (NSE listed) proposes to raise INR 750 crore via QIP to fund road BOT projects. Current promoter holding 52%; proposed QIP dilution of approximately 8%. Company has appointed a merchant banker (ICICI Securities) and legal counsel. Please provide: (1) Eligibility checks under Reg 172 of SEBI ICDR (equity listed for 1 year, compliance with LODR, no SEBI debarments), (2) Board and special resolution requirements (15-month validity), (3) Placement document preparation key contents, (4) Floor price calculation using 2-week/26-week higher VWAP formula with permitted 5% discount, (5) Allocation rules (minimum 10% to MFs, maximum 50% to single QIB, minimum 2 allottees for issues below INR 250 crore), (6) Placement document filing with SEBI and exchanges, (7) Lock-in on shares (not applicable to QIP except for related party allotments), (8) Post-issue filings and utilisation monitoring, (9) Stock exchange intimations under Reg 30, (10) In-principle and listing approvals. Format the output as a transaction timeline and compliance memo. Consider constraints: SEBI ICDR Chapter VI, LODR, and Companies Act Section 42/62.
